mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal a dual monetary policy challenge, which destabilizes equity markets and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exceed their thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 30% of instances where both INDPRO and HOUST exceed their thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects strong labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 35% of instances where PAYEMS rises by > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in residential construction (RSAFS) signals weak demand for housing, which depresses equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS falls below 340,000.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a supply-side shock, which creates inflationary pressures and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO rises and GDPC1 falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer demand (HOUST) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4%) indicates resilient economic activity, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 35% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% and HOUST remains above 2000.
